name: Security Engineer Pack
description: Workflows for application security engineers: threat modeling, OWASP vulnerability review, secrets scanning, dependency auditing, secure design patterns, and security incident response.

codex.notes: Security skills and portable managed threat-modeling prompts/templates/tools validate for Codex and local coding-agent workflows.

claude-code.notes: Agent Skills and portable managed security capabilities are compatible for Claude Code users.

recommendation.reason: Useful early pack for application security review and threat modeling.

useCases: Review application changes for threat-model gaps, unsafe input handling, secrets exposure, and dependency risk.
Create secure design guidance for APIs, auth flows, data access, logging, and operational controls.
Triage vulnerabilities with severity, exploitability, compensating controls, and owner/date remediation steps.
Help local coding agents apply security hardening without broadening the change beyond the risk being fixed.
examplePrompts: Review this code change for application security risks, OWASP issues, secrets exposure, and missing tests.
Create a threat model for this feature, including assets, trust boundaries, abuse cases, mitigations, and open decisions.
Triage these dependency or scanner findings and separate urgent fixes from accepted or deferred risk.
Design a secure implementation plan for this API, covering auth, authorization, validation, logging, and rollback.

capabilities.name: Security engineer pack guidance
capabilities.content: Use the Security Engineer Pack to help local coding agents find and fix concrete application security risk. Focus on trust boundaries, authn/authz, input validation, secrets, dependency risk, logging, data exposure, operational controls, and tests that prove the mitigation.

capabilities.name: Application security review
capabilities.content: Review this change for application security risk. Check authn/authz, input validation, injection, SSRF, XSS, CSRF, secrets exposure, unsafe logging, dependency risk, data access, privacy exposure, and missing tests. Prioritize exploitable findings and smallest safe fixes.
